subject: DevOps
title: Policy as Code
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Policy as Code.

hook.text: The security team said: 'No public databases.' An engineer created one anyway. Nobody checked until the audit.

buildup.text: Written policies in documents get ignored. If the policy isn't enforced automatically, it's just a suggestion.

discovery.text: Policy as code writes security and compliance rules in a machine-readable language. OPA's Rego is the most popular.

twist.text: Policies run in the CI pipeline. If a change violates a rule, the pipeline fails. The engineer sees exactly why.

climax.text: HashiCorp Sentinel and AWS Config Rules are alternatives. All enforce rules automatically, not through willpower.

punchline.text: Write policies in code. Enforce them in the pipeline. Compliance automated.

quiz.question: Why is policy-as-code better than written policy documents?
options: Policies are enforced automatically in the pipeline
Code policies are optional and less strict
